name: Masa Sovada (Portuguese Sweetbread)
recipeIngredient: 1/4 cup Water (warm for dry; lukewarm for compressed)
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 350 degrees.
2. Dissolve yeast in warm water.
3. Scald milk and add to sugar, butter, and salt; stir until butter is melted.
4. Mix cinnamon and flour.
5. Add half the flour to the milk/sugar/butter/salt mixture, and mix until smooth.
6. Beat 3 eggs and add them and the yeast to the mixture.
7. Continue adding remaining flour to make a soft dough.
8. Remove dough from the bowl and place on a floured board.
9. Knead until smooth and satiny (about 15 minutes).
10. Shape into a ball and place in a buttered bowl.
11. Cover and let rise until double in size (2-1/2 to 3 hours).
12. Punch down risen dough and divide it in half.
13. Place halves in two greased pans (8 inches round).
14. Let rise in a warm place until double in size (1-1/2 to 2 hours).
15. Brush tops of dough with remaining egg (beaten).
16. Bake in the 350 degree oven for 20-30 minutes or until golden brown.
17. Cool on wire racks.
